items.title: Is a Chinese invasion of Taiwan imminent?
items.description: 🇺🇸🇨🇳After nearly a decade since his last visit, US President Donald Trump is meeting Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing in a historic summit focused on global trade, technological rivalry and rising tensions over Taiwan, one of the world’s most sensitive flashpoints. 🇹🇼China claims Taiwan as part of its territory while the United States continues to back the island with military support and strong diplomatic ties. Caught in the middle is a resilient democracy that has become one of Asia’s greatest success stories, despite never being formally recognised as a sovereign state by most other countries. 🎥FRANCE 24's Stella Elgersma takes a closer look at the tense triangle between Taiwan, China and the United States: how it came to be, what's really at stake and whether a military confrontation is truly on the horizon.

items.title: News24 | Israel threatens New York Times lawsuit over ‘most hideous and distorted lies’
items.description: Israel threatened to take The New York Times to court over a piece it published denouncing allegedly widespread sexual abuse against Palestinian detainees.

items.title: South Korea says it will pursue all options to avoid Samsung strike

items.description: Health officials in Democratic Republic of Congo have intensified emergency response measure after an Ebola outbreak in Ituri province claimed at least 80 lives, raising fears of further spread.

items.title: Relief for Milei as inflation in Argentina slows for first time in 11 months

items.title: Poll shows women’s football growing in popularity and reach in Argentina
items.description: New study reveals 30% of Argentines play football at least once a month, with one in four females taking to the pitch in past 12 months. The women’s game is developing, but growth has yet to translate into improved professionalism and sponsorship opportunities. Leer más

items.title: UK: Health Secretary Wes Streeting resigns, likely in bid to topple PM Keir Starmer
items.description: Two Labour Party politicians have positioned themselves to challenge Prime Minister Keir Starmer for the leadership. Health Secretary Wes Streeting resigned, and Angela Rayner said her tax affairs were back in order.

items.title: Iranian man barely survives bombing, but wants war to go on
items.description: An Iranian man who was buried under rubble in US Israel bombing campaign says he wants the war to go on, and destroy the regime.

items.description: We need a proper contest with the best candidates on the field, and I'll be standing, says Wes Streeting

items.title: Fernando Tarapow, Argentine defender of Antarctica and Arctic, nominated for prestigious Shackleton Medal prize
items.description: Fernando Tarapow seems to have lived several lives already – a member of the Argentine Navy for four decades, he is also a lawyer, professor of international law and polar expedition guide. Now, he’s been nominated on the “longlist” for the prestigious Shackleton Medal, an international recognition awarded to those who work to protect Antarctica and the Arctic. Leer más

items.title: The war in Ukraine is at a standstill as societal fatigue grows in Russia
items.description: With the peace process stalled, there are signs of growing public fatigue in Russia with the Kremlin's war as Ukraine uses robotic warfare to stay in the fight.

items.title: 'Regime change': Hungary's Magyar exposes Orban's decadence

items.title: Islamic feminism more widespread in Bosnia and Herzegovina

items.title: Syrian Foreign Minister makes first visit to Morocco since Assad’s fall
items.description: Morocco and Syria have taken a major step toward restoring relations, as Syrian Foreign Minister Assaad al-Chaibani visited Rabat for talks with Moroccan Foreign Minister Nasser Bourita. The visit marks the first by a senior Syrian official to Morocco since the fall of Bashar al-Assad.

items.title: India news: CNG gas price hiked in Delhi NCR amid Iran war fallout
items.description: The price of CNG has increased in Delhi and the surrounding region for the second time in two days, as India grapples with the economic impact of the Iran war. Meanwhile, Modi is set to arrive in Sweden. DW has more.

items.title: OAPs: Old Age Presidents
items.description: US presidents Donald Trump and Joe Biden were both criticised for being too old to serve as heads of government, as has Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva in Brazil. But India’s Narendra Modi, at the youthful age of 75, is also older and does not face the same questioning. Old age appears to hold a different value, depending on the culture in question. But is old age an obstacle to being a good leader? Leer más
